food_subgroup: Ice cream and frozen dairy desserts

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard scoop of vanilla classic ice cream is approximately 100 g, equivalent to a typical single-serve portion at a dessert counter or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Vanilla Classic Ice Cream — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Vanilla Classic (cr_id G00010464), confidence 0.95. All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — ice cream, regular, vanilla
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 61 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular full-fat vanilla ice cream (glucose reference = 100). Glycemic load calculated as GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 61 × 22.29 / 100 = 13.6 per 100 g serving. This is the closest approved analogous entry for a full-fat vanilla dairy ice cream with comparable sugar and fat profile.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (20.65 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (~9.8 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit vanilla classic ice cream due to its high sugar content (20.65 g/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~9.8 g/100 g), both of which elevate blood pressure through insulin-mediated sodium retention and vascular stiffness; a 100 g serving delivers approximately 31 g combined sugar and saturated fat load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 20.65 g/100 g with 0 g dietary fibre produces rapid postprandial glucose excursions; medium GI (61) combined with high sugar density is clinically significant for glycaemic management
type2_diabetes.guidance: Vanilla classic ice cream is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar content (20.65 g/100 g), zero dietary fibre, and medium glycemic index (61), which together cause rapid postprandial glucose spikes and worsen insulin resistance; it should be avoided or replaced with a sugar-free, lower-fat frozen dessert alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.8 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g cardiovascular threshold; cholesterol at 92 mg/100 g and high sugar further elevate LDL and triglyceride risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Vanilla classic ice cream poses a high cardiovascular risk due to its estimated saturated fat content (~9.8 g/100 g), cholesterol (92 mg/100 g), and high sugar (20.65 g/100 g), all of which contribute to elevated LDL cholesterol and triglycerides; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and choose lower-fat, lower-sugar alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined sugar at 20.65 g/100 g exceeds the RA gate (>15 g/100 g); saturated fat (~9.8 g/100 g) promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid vanilla classic ice cream as its high refined sugar (20.65 g/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~9.8 g/100 g) promote systemic inflammatory mediators including TNF-alpha and IL-6, which worsen joint inflammation and RA symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vanilla Classic Ice Cream is a full-fat dairy frozen dessert made from a creamy blend of cream, milk, sugar, egg yolk, and vanilla extract. It is produced by churning and freezing a custard base, resulting in a smooth, rich, and dense texture with a pronounced sweet vanilla flavour and a cold, melt-in-the-mouth mouthfeel. Per 100 g, it provides 249 kcal, 16.2 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~9.8 g), 22.29 g carbohydrates, 20.65 g sugar, 3.5 g protein, and 0 g dietary fibre. The sodium content is 61 mg/100 g, calcium is 117 mg/100 g, and cholesterol is 92 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is approximately 61 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of ~13.6 per 100 g serving. Clinically, this food is high concern for Type 2 Diabetes (high sugar, zero fibre, medium GI), hypertension (high sugar drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat contributes to arterial stiffness), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat and cholesterol elevate LDL). It is also pro-inflammatory and unsuitable for rheumatoid arthritis management. Vanilla ice cream is widely consumed as a dessert or snack in Western and international food contexts, and is commonly served in Singapore as a standalone scoop or accompaniment to warm desserts such as waffles or lava cake.
common_user_queries: How many calories in vanilla ice cream?
Can diabetics eat vanilla ice cream?
Is vanilla ice cream high in sugar?
Is vanilla ice cream suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010464) with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. Saturated fat is estimated at ~9.8 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central data for comparable full-fat vanilla ice cream (USDA SR Legacy item 19095), as the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular vanilla ice cream.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 19095 (Ice creams, vanilla) as the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat breakdown; this estimate is consistent with the total fat of 16.2 g/100 g and the cream/egg yolk composition. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and full micronutrient panel are not available in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product, nor in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 19095 for all these specific micronutrients. 3. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — vanilla ice cream contains no plant fibre by formulation. 4. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Grains and staples' to 'Desserts' as vanilla ice cream is a frozen dairy dessert, not a grain or staple; food_subgroup updated accordingly. 5. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular full-fat vanilla ice cream; no Singapore-specific GI measurement was found in approved sources.
